tracks.meaning: to search into or examine something thoroughly and carefully
a device or instrument used to explore, test, or obtain information about something
an unmanned spacecraft designed to travel into space and collect scientific data
a thin instrument inserted into the body to examine or treat a condition
to search an area or conduct reconnaissance to gather intelligence
to touch or prod someone repeatedly in a way that annoys or provokes them
a formal inquiry or investigation into a matter of public concern
a substance or marker used to detect or track another substance in a reaction
to poke or push something with a finger or pointed object
to broach a topic tentatively to gauge someone's reaction or interest
